headline: Intern Accidentally Replies-All To Company-Wide Email, Apologizes With 3 More Reply-Alls
summary: The thread now has 400 replies and its own HR subcommittee.
fullBody: Kevin Fumblebuck, 22, accidentally hit 'reply all' to a company-wide email with the message 'lol k.' Panicking, he sent 'sorry that was for my friend' — also reply-all. Then 'please ignore' — reply-all. Then 'I'm going to delete my email' — reply-all. IT has now disabled his account. The email thread has been immortalized in company lore and will likely be studied by future generations as 'how to get fired with style.'
